summary: The author secured 2nd place by implementing a multi-stage pipeline for robust image matching and 3D reconstruction. The approach combines multiple feature detectors (SuperPoint, ALIKE, SIFT) with descriptor matchers (LightGlue, Nearest Neighbor) across both full-resolution images and targeted crops. Matches are concatenated, filtered via RANSAC with a strict threshold, and fed into COLMAP to generate the final reconstruction and clusters.
extensive_summary:
## Feature Extraction
All input images are resized so that the longest side is 1280 pixels. The pipeline begins by extracting features using SuperPoint (utilizing a GIM checkpoint) and filtering initial pairs with a minimum of 30 matches. These pairs are then processed with ALIKE and LightGlue, alongside a parallel branch using SIFT with Nearest Neighbor matching.

## Crop Generation and Matching
Crops for the target object are generated based on the ensemble of matches from the initial full-image extraction. The pipeline applies ALIKE + LightGlue and SuperPoint + LightGlue to these crops to capture finer-grained correspondences that may be missed at the full-image scale.

## Final Matching and Reconstruction
Matches from all stages (full-image and crop-based pipelines) are concatenated. A RANSAC filter is applied, retaining only pairs with a minimum of 150 matches to ensure geometric consistency. The filtered matches are passed to COLMAP with default parameters to reconstruct the scene and generate clusters, following the methodology of a public reference notebook.
competition_flow: Images are resized to a 1280px longest side, processed through an ensemble of SuperPoint, ALIKE, and SIFT feature extractors with LightGlue/NN matchers on full images and crops, filtered via RANSAC, and reconstructed using COLMAP.
data_processing: All images resized to longest side of 1280 pixels
Crops generated for target object based on ensemble of matches
ensembling: Matches from multiple feature extraction pipelines (SuperPoint, ALIKE, SIFT) on both full images and generated crops are concatenated and filtered via RANSAC with a minimum of 150 matches before reconstruction.
insights: Combining multiple feature detectors across different scales significantly improves match coverage.
Processing targeted crops alongside full images captures fine-grained details critical for accurate reconstruction.
Applying a strict RANSAC match threshold (150) is essential for filtering out geometric outliers before COLMAP processing.
